2000-04-25.abstract: Reformers in Iran, two months after their triumph in parliamentary elections, have come under fierce pressure from powerful Islamic conservatives; hard-liners have jailed writers and annulled some election results; Tehran courts have ordered eight daily and four weekly newspapers to stop publishing; banned newspapers that supported Pres Mohammad Khatami, whose calls for reform of judiciary and for open political debate have won him broad public support, but also fierce enemies in clerical establishment; photo (M)

2000-04-25.title: Liquica Journal; East Timor's New Issue: Can It Forgive and Forget?
2000-04-25.abstract: Former members of violent anti-independence militias are returning to Liquica and other towns in East Timor from their refuge in Indonesian West Timor to beg forgiveness from survivors; map; photo (M)

2000-04-25.title: Dispute Could Warm U.S.-Cuba Relations
2000-04-25.abstract: Experts say that in immediate sense, hostility in Cuban-American official relations is unlikely to be touched by Elian Gonzalez case; say that in longer term, dispute could encourage trends in American public opinion that favor more relaxed ties (M)

2000-04-26.title: Andre Jarrot, 90, Co-Leader Of French Resistance Group
Missile Defense May Have Price Of $60 Billion
2000-04-26.abstract: Congressional Budget Office estimates cost of building national missile defense system would be nearly $60 billion; graph; Pentagon, which does not dispute figure, has only one more chance to prove that technology works before Pres Clinton makes decision this summer; one of two tests of full system has succeeded so far (S)

2000-04-26.title: Zhdanovo Journal; Intoxicated With Religion (and Quite Literally)
2000-04-26.abstract: Zhdanovo Journal on region in southern Siberia that is outpost of mystical Mongol religion, shamanism; villagers consider vodka holy water to be sprinkled and, most of all, drunk as integral part of religious rites; scene in one village described; photo; map (M)

2000-04-26.title: Colombia Agrees to Turn Over Territory to Another Rebel Group
2000-04-26.abstract: Colombia agrees to withdraw troops from remote northern region, hoping to advance peace talks aimed at ending 35 years of fighting; Pres Andres Pastrana does not specify timing of second such withdrawal, which would create safe haven for National Liberation Army guerrillas (M)

2000-04-26.title: Rightist in Canada's West Has Eyes Set on Ottawa
2000-04-26.abstract: Alberta treasurer Stockwell Day presides over fiscal revolution that has eliminated province's net debt, cut income taxes to Canada's lowest levels and plans to impose Canada's first flat tax, all in social welfare nation that has more tax collectors than soldiers; Day is also moving to expand conservative movement to rest of Canada, hoping to become prime minister next year; photo; he is also only French speaker of major leadership candidates and appears to want to soften Christian Alliance's anti-Quebec stance (M)

2000-04-27.title: Tokyo Journal; TV Invents What Japan Sorely Lacks: Impoliteness
2000-04-27.abstract: One of Japan's most popular television programs is Strange Things About the Japanese, weekly show in which Japanese-speaking foreigners mix idiocy and bully boy cruelty to criticize life in Japan and Japanese people in general; show slyly uses foreigners to criticize aspects of society that much of Japanese news media treats with kid gloves; show works as Japanese entertainment, unthreateningly highlighting country's unusual situation as island of high prosperity inhabited by provincial, nearly homogenous and inwardly turned people; photo (M)

2000-04-27.title: U.S. SAYS RUSSIANS MAY WANT A DEAL ON MISSILE DEFENSE
2000-04-27.abstract: Senior Clinton administration officials hold second round of intensive meetings with Russian Foreign Min Igor S Ivanov about missile defense system United States wants to develop over Russian objections; say Ivanov and chief Russian arms control negotiator, Georgi Memedev, continue to oppose plan, but that they detect readiness by Moscow to explore all avenues of possible resolution to looming standoff; State Dept describes its three-prong approach: no deferral of missile defense issue, no destruction of Antiballistic Missile Treaty of 1972, which limits ability of both US and Russia to deploy defenses against missiles, and no decoupling from Start III, which would cut number of nuclear warheads in each country's arsenal; Sen Jesse Helms and other leading conservative Republicans want ABM treaty scrapped to allow for more robust missile defense than Clinton administration is proposing (M)

2000-04-27.title: McCain, in Vietnam, Finds the Past Isn't Really Past
2000-04-27.abstract: Sen John McCain visits building in Hanoi, Vietnam, where he spent five years as prisoner of war, including two years in solitary confinement; McCain, long a proponent of better relations with Vietnam, betrays feelings still raw, 25 years later, at how captors treated his fellow servicemen; McCain meets with senior Vietnamese officials to discuss expanded trade and other matters; war is shadow that lurks behind every meeting (M)

2000-04-27.title: Orphans, the Most Rootless of Chechnya's Uprooted
2000-04-27.abstract: War in Chechnya drags on, and temporary dislocations seem to have become semipermanent; Chechnya still has not elected leaders because situation is too unstable to arange local elections; refugee camps are overflowing, and refugees--living four families to a tent--say their homes either have been destroyed or are still in battle zone; refugees daring to leave camp to check on their homes risk missing their food rations; orphanage in Grozny that was forced to move to town of Nadterechnoye has 29 children who now live in cramped quarters with little food, furniture or clothing; photos (M)

2000-04-27.title: Bush Debates Foreign Policy With Russian
2000-04-27.abstract: Gov George W Bush meets with Russian Foreign Min Igor Ivanov, reportedly at Ivanov's request, to air differences over missile defense system and Chechnya; Bush expresses hope that US and Russia can work together to keep peace in world; Bush, accompanied by several foreign policy advisers, says talks ranged from discussion of Pres-elect Vladimor V Putin's goals for his country, to United States investment in Russia, to American interest in developing antimissile defense system; like many other Republicans, Bush says he would back out of 1972 ABM treaty to free US to pursue its own course, should Russia refuse to amend that treaty; photo (M)

2000-04-28.title: Argentina's President Wins Reform of Rigid Labor Code
2000-04-28.abstract: Opposition-controlled Argentine Senate gives Pres Fernando de la Rua a much-needed lift by passing his labor reform bill, which is intended to lower 14 percent unemployment rate by reducing labor costs for businesses and curtailing power of big labor; overwhelming vote of approval caps six weeks of intense political maneuvering and sometimes violent labor union protests that made new de la Rua government look increasingly unadept as cabinet ministers publicly second-guessed themselves (M)

2000-04-28.title: Beijing Jails Muslim Woman for Sending Newspapers Abroad
2000-04-28.abstract: Newly available transcript of court verdict confirms that Muslim businesswoman, Rebiya Kadeer, was dealt an eight-year prison sentence mainly because she tried to mail her exiled husband back copies of local newspapers; Kadeer, 53, was convicted on March 10 in her home province of Xinjian in western China for 'revealing state intelligence' abroad (M)

2000-04-28.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-04-28.abstract: ASIA    CHINA: NO PAROLE FOR RESEARCHER -- The Chinese government rejected a request for medical parole for a Chinese-born Stanford University researcher who has been jailed in Beijing since 1998 on charges of revealing military secrets, a Hong Kong human rights group said. Hua Di, a former Chinese weapons scientist who is serving 15 years, has a rare form of breast cancer that strikes men. A Beijing Court recently ordered that Mr. Hua be retried, saying that his conviction was based on ''unclear facts.''   Elisabeth Rosenthal (NYT)

2000-04-28.title: Proposal on ABM: 'Ready to Work With Russia'
2000-04-28.abstract: Excerpts from document that America negotiators have presented to Russians with proposals for amending 1972 antiballistic missile treaty, in order to alow United States to build limited national missile defense system (M)

2000-04-28.title: 300 People, 14 Dead, Found Stranded on Isle in Bahamas
2000-04-28.abstract: Fourteen Haitians die and 264 others are stranded on uninhabited island in Bahamas for unknown period of time after their overloaded 50-foot boat breaks down at sea about 200 miles southeast of Nassau; Bahamian and American vessels participate in rescue effort; Haitians have long come to Bahamas to seek work in menial jobs in gardening, construction or housecleaning, fleeing poverty and violence that have crippled their homeland for years; map (M)

2000-04-29.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-04-29.abstract: EUROPE    KOSOVO: U.N. DELEGATION VISITS -- An eight-member delegation from the United Nations Security Council, including the ambassadors from China and Russia, visited Mitrovica on a two-day tour of Kosovo to observe the United Nations mission at work and send a strong message to local communities to reject violence. Guarded by special troops and with a bodyguard behind each ambassador, Dr. Bernard Kouchner, above, the head of the mission, led the delegation on a brief walk across the bridge that links the city's Serb-dominated north with the Albanian south. The bridge has been the scene of much recent violence.     Carlotta Gall (NYT)

2000-04-29.title: Execution Frays Canada-Vietnam Relations
2000-04-29.abstract: Canada withdraws ambassador from Vietnam to protest Vietnam's execution of Vietnamese-born Canadian woman, Nguyen Thi Hiep, for drug trafficking; will also boycott ceremony marking 25th anniversary of end of Vietnam War (S)

2000-04-29.title: A Fund Is Planned By U.S. Companies For Nazis' Victims
2000-04-29.abstract: Several American companies that had factories in Nazi Germany will set up fund under auspices of US Chamber of Commerce to aid people who suffered persecution under Hitler; aim is at least in part to head off lawsuits; Clinton administration has pressed for fund to help aging survivors; Deputy Sec Stuart Eizenstat comments; at least 50 American companies operated factories in Nazi Germany; most were nationalized after 1941, but were returned just after war; some companies received dividends or benefited from forced labor (M)

2000-04-29.title: Right's Anti-ABM Weapon: A Senate Clause on Russia
2000-04-29.abstract: Senate conservatives trying to scuttle Pres Clinton's limited national defense missile system use little-known weapon of clause they inserted into separate 1997 pact, which leaves it to Senate to decide whether Russia inherited Soviet role in 1972 ABM treaty; Russia is treated as legal successor to Soviet Union in all other matters, but Sen Jesse Helms wrote to Pres Clinton two years ago suggesting that even US is no longer party to AMB treaty (M)

2000-04-29.title: Political Violence at Home Made Haitians Flee, Boat Survivors Say
2000-04-29.abstract: Survivors of shipwreck that stranded more than 250 Haitians on barren Bahamas island say they fled in desperate attempt to escape violence sweeping Haiti in advance of elections; 222 survivors arrive in Nassau on Bahamian naval vessel and recall ordeal and deaths of others; photo; map; more than 300 other Haitians have been rescued in Bahamas in recent weeks, alarming government that cannot handle influx (M)

2000-04-30.title: Cambodia Agrees to Tribunal Setup for Khmer Rouge Trials
Mozambique Confronts Huge Flood Losses but Sees Ray of Hope
2000-04-30.abstract: Flood waters are slowly receding nearly two months after torrential rains ravaged Mozambique; United Nations and Mozambique report nation must raise about $427 million for reconstruction, which is only just beginning; put death toll at 700; UN aid official Mark Malloch Brown comments (M)

2000-04-30.title: U.N. Human Rights Panel Faults Russia and Others, but Not China
2000-04-30.abstract: United Nations Human Rights Commission, ending six-week session, takes strong stand against Russia's actions in Chechnya, while failing to condemn China's repression of political and spiritual dissidents; censures several other nations (M)

2000-04-30.title: U.N. Delegation Winds Up Visit to Kosovo
2000-04-30.abstract: The eight-member delegation from the United Nations Security Council, which came to Kosovo to give a stern message to all sides to stop the violence and to urge on the United Nations mission here, wound up its visit today somewhat muted by the complexities on the ground.    ''Visiting areas around Kosovo has made us realize how enormous the task is,'' said the chairman of the delegation, Anwarul Karim Chowdhury of Bangladesh, describing the United Nations mission in Kosovo. ''When we passed the resolution,'' creating the mission, he added, the United Nations ambassadors ''had no idea how involved the mission's officials would be in day-to-day affairs.''

2000-04-30.title: Advertising Giants Call Off Merger Talks
